departments.name: Administration Company
departments.brief: Administration Company is a department of Headquarters Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo, charged with coordinating the training support activities of the institution. It enforces discipline among all soldiers of the Depot and is responsible for its internal security, conducting routine day and night patrols that have also contributed to improved security in the surrounding host communities.
departments.hod_bio:
Major Nurudeen Ibraheem was born on 7 September 1989 in Offa Local Government Area, Kwara State. He attended Nawair-Ud-Deen Primary School for his primary education, followed by Offa Grammar School, Offa (Kwara State) and Okoyelola High School, Konta-Ijabe (Osun State) for his secondary education.

He was admitted to the Nigerian Defence Academy on 14 August 2010 as a member of 62 Regular Course and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Corps of Infantry on 12 September 2015.

Major Ibraheem has served in various units and appointments including: Platoon Commander and Acting Operations Officer, 19 Battalion Okitipupa (Nov 2015 – Nov 2016); Operations Officer, Intelligence Officer, Battalion Adjutant and Company Commander (Alfa and Bravo Companies), 81 Division Task Force Battalion, Gulumba-Gana and Dikwa, Borno State (2016 – 2020); Battalion Adjutant, 2 Battalion Kaduna (from Mar 2020); Pioneer Operations Officer, 2 Special Women Operations Battalion Abakaliki; and Officer Commanding, Administration Company, Depot NA Osogbo (current).

Courses attended include Young Officers Course 26/17, Basic Surveillance Course 11/23 and Junior Course 95/23, all at the Nigerian Army School of Infantry Jaji, as well as Basic Human Rights Awareness Course 7/22 at the Martin Luther Agwai Leadership and Peacekeeping Centre Jaji.

He is married and blessed with three children. His hobbies include reading, jogging and listening to music.

members.name: Maj N Ibraheem - Administration Company-1
members.bio:
ADMINISTRATION COMPANY
Administration Company (Admin Company) is a department of the Depot Headquarters. It is charged with coordinating the training support activities of the institution. It also ensures the security of the Depot. To this end, a routine day and night patrol is being conducted. This, by extension has also improved the security of the host communities. It also enforces discipline among the soldiers of the Depot.
Major Nurudeen Ibraheem is the Officer Commanding Admin Company. He was born on 7th September 1989 in Offa Local Government Area, where he attended Nawair-Ud-Deen Primary School. He had his Secondary education at Offa Grammar School, Offa Kwara State and Okoyelola High School, Konta-Ijabe, Osun State. On 14th
August 2010, Nurudeen got admitted into the Nigerian Defence Academy as a member of 62 Regular Course and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Corp of Infantry on 12 September 2015.
He has served in various units where he held different appointments. These includes 19 Battalion Okitipupa as a Platoon Commander and Acting Operations Officer between November 2015 – November 2016. 81 Divisaion Task Force Battalion (81 Div TF Bn) Gulumba-Gana and Dikwa in Borno State from 2016 – 2020. At 81 Div TF Bn, he was appointed Operations Officer, Intelligence Officer and the Battalion Adjutant. He also commanded Alfa and Bravo Companies of the Battalion at various times. In March 2020, he was taken on strength at 2 Battalion, Kaduna and appointed the Battalion Adjutant in August.
He was posted as the pioneer operation officer of 2 Special Women Operations Battalion,Abakaliki on 11 January 2011, he was posted to Depot NA Osogbo.
However, following his promotion to the rank of Major in August the same year, he was redeployed to Admin Coy as the Officer Commanding.
Nurudeen has attended a number of courses which include Young Officers Course 26/17, Basic Surveillance Course 11/23 and Junior Course 95/23. All at the Nigerian Army School of Infantry, Jaji, Kaduna State. Other courses attended includes, Basic Human Right Awareness Course 7/22 at the Martin Lurther Leadership and Peace Keeping Centre also in Jaji among other courses.
He is married and blessed with 3 Children. In his leisure time, he enjoys reading, jugging and listening to the music of Dr SikiruAyinde Barrister.

departments.name: Army Public Relations
departments.brief:
The Army Public Relations Department was established on 31 May 2025 as the branch directly responsible to the Commandant for all public relations activities of Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. The department manages information to project the image and reputation of the institution, provides media advisory to the Commandant and serves as the official liaison and protocol office of the Depot. Its current manpower holding is 1 x 6.

The department's functions include managing relationships with the media and ensuring all public communications positively reflect the institution; coordinating press releases, official statements and responses to media inquiries; overseeing the management of digital communication platforms and the institution's social media presence; enforcing Nigerian Army protocols on online communications; publicising Depot activities; producing and distributing banners, flyers and other print media; liaising with civil authorities and paramilitary organisations; conducting and coordinating all visits to the institution; receiving and ushering guests to the Commandant's office; and providing photo and video coverage of all Depot activities.
departments.hod_role: Deputy Assistant Director, Army Public Relations
departments.hod_bio:
Major Yahaya Arama Ibrahim was born on 29 September 1983 into the family of Late Mallam Abdulrahim Ibrahim. He hails from Okene Local Government Area of Kogi State.

He began his primary education at Command Children's School, Dantuku Road, Kaduna (1986 – 1992) and proceeded to Nigerian Military School Zaria (1992 – 1998) for his secondary education. He obtained a National Diploma and Higher National Diploma in Mass Communication from Kaduna Polytechnic (2000 – 2006) and a Post Graduate Diploma in Mass Communication from Ahmadu Bello University Zaria in 2010.

Major Ibrahim was commissioned into the Nigerian Army as a Lieutenant on 5 July 2014 as a member of Direct Short Service Course 21. He is an Associate Member of the Nigerian Institute of Public Relations.

He has served in the following appointments: SO3 Public Administration and Research, HQ 2 Division APR; Force Public Information Officer, United Nations Mission in Liberia; SO3 Publicity and Information, HQ 81 Division APR; Liaison Officer, NAOWA 81 Division Chapter; ADAPR, HQ 29 Task Force Brigade Maiduguri; Ag ADAPR, HQ 1 Brigade Gusau; Force Information Officer, JTF North West Op HADARIN DAJI; Information Officer, Op SAHEL SANITY Katsina; Ag ADAPR, NACEST Makurdi; Pioneer ADAPR, Depot NA Osogbo (current).

Courses attended include Young Officers Course Army Public Relations, Media Operations Officers Course and Advanced Public Relations Course (all at NASPR); BMATT CIMIC Operators Course, Martin Luther Agwai International Peacekeeping Centre Jaji; Basic Computer Course, Nigerian Army Signals School Apapa; and Junior Leadership and Staff Officers Course 1/27, NACOLAM Lagos.

His operational experience includes United Nations Mission in Liberia (NIBATT 26, 2013; NIBATT 38, 2016), Operation Lafiya Dole Maiduguri (2018 – 2020), Operation Sharan Daji / Operation Hadarin Daji Zamfara (2020 – 2024), Operation Forest Sanity Katsina (2020) and Operation Sahel Sanity Sokoto (2021).

departments.name: Catering Department
departments.brief:
The Catering Department was established at the inception of Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo in July 2025 to provide nutritional support essential to recruit training. The department's primary responsibility is the provision of three square meals daily for recruits in line with extant Nigerian Army regulations on rations. In addition, all Depot personnel receive weekday lunch in accordance with the COAS directive.

The department works with vetted food vendors who supply dry rations and engages civilian caterers to supplement Nigerian Army personnel. Currently, 80 civilian staff operate in two shifts alongside Supply and Transport personnel to support catering services.
departments.hod_bio:
Lieutenant Colonel A.A. Amadi hails from Agba-Ozizza Community in Afikpo North Local Government Area of Ebonyi State.

He attended Agba Primary School for his primary education and Comprehensive Secondary School Afikpo for his secondary education. He thereafter proceeded to Federal Polytechnic Unwana-Afikpo where he obtained a National Diploma and Higher National Diploma in Accounting and Finance. He was admitted to the Nigerian Defence Academy and commissioned as a member of Short Service Course 34.

Upon commissioning, he was posted to Headquarters Nigerian Army Corps of Supply and Transport, followed by a posting to 1 Division Transport Battalion as Platoon Commander. He was subsequently nominated for NIBATT 10 African Mission in Sudan and thereafter NIBATT 19 United Nations Mission in Liberia (UNMIL). Following his de-induction from UNMIL, he deployed to the North East as Catering Officer, 3 Division Special Operations Brigade, which later became 27 Task Force Brigade. He attended the Armed Forces Command and Staff College for his Junior Course, followed by a posting to NACOL as Catering Officer. He served as Acting CO, Supply and Transport Battalion, 4 Brigade Benin and attended the AFCSC Senior Course. Subsequent postings include SO2 Policy, AHQ DAPP; Commanding Officer, 763 Supply and Transport Battalion; DCOS G4, HQ NACST (December 2025); and Catering Officer, Depot NA Osogbo (January 2026 – current).

He is married to Mrs Augusta Ubolo Amadi-Ulu and the union is blessed with two children. His hobbies include swimming, playing badminton and watching nature movies.

members.name: Lt Col Aa Amadi
members.bio:
Lt Col AA Amadi hails from Agba-Ozizza Community in Afikpo North LGA of Ebonyi State. He attended Agba Primary School where he obtained his First School Leaving Certificate and then proceeded to Comprehensive Secondary School, Afikpo for his Senior School Certificate. He thereafter gained admission to the Federal Polytechnic, Unwana-Afikpo where he obtained his National Diploma  and Higher National Diploma in Accounting and Finance.
The Senior Officer was admitted into the NDA where he trained and was commissioned as a member of Short Service Course 34. Upon completion of his cadet training, he was posted to Headquarters Nigerian Army Corps of Supply and Transport (HQ NACST). The Senior Officer was thereafter posted to 	1 Div Tpt Bn as a Pl Comd from where he was nominated as part of NIBATT 10 African Mission in Sudan for peace support operations. Upon return from the peace support operations, Lt Col Amadi was nominated as part of NIBATT 19, United Nations Mission in Liberia (NUMIL).
Lt Col Amadi was depolyed to the NE as Cat Offr 3 Div SOB  on his de-induction from UNMIL as part of the first set of troops to counter insurgency in the NE. The unit later metamorphosed to 27 TF Bde and moved from Damaturu to Buni Yadi. After 3 years in the NE, the Senior Officer was de-inducted and proceeded to the Armed Forces Command and Staff College (AFCSC) for his Junior Course (JC). On completion of JC, Lt Col Amadi was posted to the NACOL as Cat Offr. In February 2017, he was posted to 4 Bde as Ag CO ST Bn. After his tour of duty in Benin, he proceeded to AFCSC for his Senior Course (SC).
On completion of SC at the AFCSC, Lt Col Amadi was posted to AHQ DAPP as SO2 Pcy. After serving for two years in AHQ DAPP, the Senior Officer was posted to 763 ST Bn as CO. He was thereafter posted from 763 ST Bn to HQ NACST in December 2025 and appointed DCOS G4. The Senior was posted to Depot NA Osogbo in January 2026 where he currently serves as Cat Offr.
Lt Col Amadi is married to Mrs Augusta Ubolo Amadi-Ulu and the marriage is blessed with two children. At his leisure, he enjoys swimming, playing Badminton and watching nature movies.

departments.name: Chaplaincy (Roman Catholic)
departments.brief:
The Department of Chaplain Services (Roman Catholic) was established at Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo in June 2025 with the posting of its pioneer chaplain. The department provides holistic spiritual, moral and psychological support to military personnel, their families and recruits in accordance with the mandate of the Directorate of Chaplain Services (Roman Catholic), Nigerian Army. Its current posted strength is 1 x 3.

The department's mandates include advising the Depot Commandant on administrative and religious matters; leading personnel, families and recruits in Sunday and weekday worship; organising Friday padre hours; providing spiritual and pastoral guidance; administering sacraments; conducting wedding ceremonies, naming ceremonies and funeral services; and offering guidance and counselling to officers, soldiers, families and recruits.
departments.hod_bio:

He was posted to 2 Division NA Ibadan as SO2 Chaplain (RC) on 17 February 2025 and subsequently to Depot NA Osogbo as Pioneer Catholic Chaplain on 30 May 2025, a position he currently holds.

departments.name: Cantonment Maintenance Group
departments.brief:
The Cantonment Maintenance Group (CMG) was established at Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo on 7 June 2025. It is responsible for the maintenance, repair and general engineering services of all Depot infrastructure and utilities, including civil works, electrical services, plumbing, carpentry, masonry, water supply, accommodation, road maintenance and power supply. The CMG provides technical support to ensure seamless execution of training activities. Its current manpower holding is 2 x 15 soldiers.

Since establishment, the CMG has completed several projects: the remodelling of the Quarter Guard; construction of a stable and a temporary loose box; installation of solar-powered street lights at the Depot and parade ground; construction of drainage systems at the Recruit Centre, Training Branch and Records Office blocks; construction of checkpoints, a boom gate and guard house using hesco bags; jacketing and remodelling of Tangos to include conveniences and rest areas; and construction of the Hall of Fame and an open kiosk.
departments.hod_bio:
Lieutenant Colonel Ishaya Shehu Lucas was born on 2 August 1983 into the family of the late WOII Lucas Shehu (RTD) and Mrs Esi Hadiza Lucas. He hails from Karim Lamido Local Government Area of Taraba State.

He thereafter proceeded to Government Science Secondary School Jalingo, where he obtained his Senior School Certificate in 2000. He is married and blessed with one son.

departments.name: Education Department
departments.hod_bio:
Major Ibrahim Adamu Tahir was born on 8 February 1988 in Kaduna into the family of Alhaji Adamu Tahir. He hails from Gombe Local Government Area of Gombe State.

He attended LGEA Sheikh Abubakar Mahmud Gumi Primary School, Kaduna for his primary education and Netherlands International Schools, Kaduna for his secondary education. He obtained a National Diploma in Mineral Resources Engineering from Kaduna Polytechnic (2007), a Bachelor of Science in Geography from Usmanu Danfodiyo University Sokoto (2011), a Post Graduate Diploma in Education from the National Teachers Institute Kaduna (2015) and a Master of Science in Geography from Kaduna State University (2025). He is a member of the Association of Nigerian Geographers, the Teachers Registration Council of Nigeria and the Nigerian Army Resource Centre.

He was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Education Corps on 5 July 2014 as a member of Direct Short Service Course 21.

He has served in the following appointments: Instructor, Nigerian Military School Zaria (2014 – 2017); Instructor, Command Science Secondary School (Girls) Gusau (2017 – 2018); Lecturer, Nigerian Army College of Education Ilorin (2020 – 2021); Supervisory Education Officer, Depot NA Zaria (July 2021); Watchkeeper, 27 Task Force Brigade Buni Gari, Yobe State (2022); Instructor, Martin Luther Agwai International Leadership and Peacekeeping Centre Jaji (January 2025); and Supervisory Education Officer, Depot NA Osogbo (April 2026 – current).

Courses attended include Young Officers Course Education, Young Officers Course Infantry, Basic and Advanced Library Courses, Basic Systems Approach to Training and Curriculum Course, Basic NAEC Officers Development Course, Junior Leadership and Staff Officers Course and United Nations Military Observers Course. He participated in Operation HADIN KAI from July 2022 to January 2025.

He is married to Bilkisu Yunusa Adam and the union is blessed with children.

members.name: Cpl Abel Yetunde Idowu
members.bio:
BRIEF BIOGRAPHY OF 13NA/70/3801 CPL OYETUNDE ABEL IDOWU
Cpl Oyetunde Abel Idowu was born on 27 November 1992 at Maun, Ipokia Local Government Area, Ogun State, Nigeria. He is an indigene of Ogun State.
He obtained the Nigeria Certificate in Education (NCE) and subsequently earned a Bachelor of Science in Education (B.Sc. Ed.) Mathematics. His academic background has equipped him with sound knowledge in education, leadership, and instructional methodologies.
Cpl Oyetunde was enlisted into the Nigerian Army through the 70 Regular Recruit Intake (70 RRI). He is presently serving at Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo, where he performs duties as an Instructor in the Map Reading Wing. In this capacity, he is responsible for training recruits and personnel in map reading, navigation, terrain appreciation, and military field orientation.
In the course of his military career, he has attended and successfully completed the following courses:
• NAEC Mustering Course – Grade A1.
• Map Reading Course – Grade A1.
• Advanced Instructional System and Technology Design Course – Grade A2.
He has also attended the following professional workshops and seminars:
• Technicalities of Teaching Students to Obtain A1 in Mathematics and WAEC/NECO System of Marking Mathematics: The Real Facts and Methodologies.
• Yorks Press and Pearson Professional Development Course on Teaching Mathematics in the 21st Century.
Cpl Oyetunde has distinguished himself in both military service and academics and has received the following awards:
• Award of Excellence as Economics Tutorial Master by Adeniran Ogunsanya College of Education, Ijanikin, Lagos State.
• Best Soldier/Teacher of the Year 2019 by Command Science Secondary School (Boys) Jega, Kebbi State.
He is a registered member of the following professional bodies:
• Teachers Registration Council of Nigeria.
• Science Teachers Association of Nigeria.
His hobbies include reading, mentoring, sports, and research. He is a firm believer in lifelong learning, self-development, discipline, and excellence.
Cpl Oyetunde Abel Idowu is single and remains committed to the ideals of the Nigerian Army through dedicated service, professional competence, and mentorship of younger generations.
Knowledge, Discipline, Service and Excellence.

departments.name: Finance Department
departments.hod_bio:
Major Musa Myek was born on 3 April 1980 into the family of Mr and Mrs Myek Magaji Dah in Jaba Local Government Area of Kaduna State.

He attended Mallam Maude Primary School, Kwoi (FSLC 1991) for his primary education and Government Secondary School Fadan-Kaje, Kaduna (O'Level 1995) for his secondary education. He obtained a Higher National Diploma in Accounting from Nuhu Bammali Polytechnic Zaria (2005) and served as a corps member of the National Youth Service Corps at the Office of the Accountant General of the Federation, Gombe State (2006 – 2007). He is a Certified National Accountant (CNA) of the Nigerian College of Accountancy Jos (2009) and a Fellow of the Institute of Leadership and Human Resources.

He was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Finance Corps on 5 July 2014 as a member of Direct Short Service Course 21.

He has served in the following appointments: Instructor, Nigerian Army School of Finance and Administration Lagos (2014 – 2015); Administrative Officer, Nigerian Army Welfare Insurance Scheme (2015 – 2016); Staff Officer, NACEST (2016 – 2017); Finance Manager, Nigerian Army Post Exchange Limited (2017 – 2019); Brigade Finance Officer, 42 Engineers Brigade (2019 – 2021); Acting Deputy Director Finance, HQ CAR Lokoja (Nov 2021 – Feb 2022); Acting SO2 Payment, HQ 8 Division Finance (2022 – 2023); Finance Officer, HQ Directorate of Legal Services Army (2024); and Pioneer Finance Officer, Depot NA Osogbo (current).

He participated in Operation HADIN KAI. Courses attended include Young Officers Course Finance (2016), Computer Appreciation Course (2019), SO2 Payment Course (2021), Junior Leadership and Staff Officers Course (2024) and the 10th and 14th Nigerian Army Finance Corps Biennial Training Conferences (2015 and 2024).

He is married to Mrs Mercy Myek Magaji. His hobbies include swimming, watching football and listening to music.

He was later posted to 2 Division Finance where he served in various department units as a finance clerk from 2006-2010. He was posted NASFA and deployed in Computer department in 2010. members.name: Wo Awodiji Olawale
members.bio:
Warrant Officer Awodiji Olawale was born on the 6th of July 1980, into the Family of Mr and Mrs JA Awodiji. He attended Command Children School, were he obtained his First School Leaving Certificate in 1992.
He joined the Nigerian Army as a boy soldier of the Nigerian Military School Zaria on the 5th of October 1993. He passed out on the 23rd of July 1999. After which he was posted to Head Quarters Command Finance Office, (HQ CFO) Apapa, Lagos.
Warrant Officer Wale Served in Head Quarters Command Finance Office (HQ CFO) Apapa as Finance Clerk from 1999 to 2002 and was later posted to 32 Field Artillery Brigade Finance (32 FAB Fin) from 2003 to 2009. In 2009, he was posted back to Head Quarters Command Finance Office (HQ CFO) Apapa as Finance Clerk, where he served from 2009 to 2016. In 2016, he was posted to Nigerian Army School of Finance and Administration, (NASFA) Apapa Lagos, as an instructor where he served from 2016 to 2018. After which he was posted to Military Pensions Board (MPB) Abuja, as Finance Clerk from 2018 to 2020. He was posted to Head Quarters 2 Division Finance and Accounts as Finance clerk in year 2020 and later Deployed to Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo as Chief Clerk, Finance Department in 2025.
Warrant Officer Wale had completed both Academic and Military Courses. He attended Finance A1 Course in Nigerian Army School of Finance and administration Apapa in 2012, VSAT Installation Course in Directorate of Army Data Processing, Bonny Camp, Lagos in 2016, and Finance A1 Refreshers Course Upgrade in Nigerian Army School of Finance and administration in 2025.
He holds a master’s degree in safety and security management (MSc), a master’s degree in business administration (MBA), a Bachelor of Science degree in Industrial Relations (BSc). Warrant Officer Wale is a member of Nigerian Institute of Management and a Fellow of Institute of Finance and Control of Nigeria. Warrant Officer Wale is happily married to Mrs Yemisi Awodiji and blessed with two children. He enjoys Drawing, Painting and Playing Chess.

departments.name: Headquarters Depot Nigerian Army
departments.brief: Headquarters Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo is the command and administrative nerve centre of the Depot. It coordinates all operational, training, administrative and logistical activities of the formation. The headquarters is headed by the Commandant, a senior officer responsible for the overall management, discipline and welfare of all personnel and recruits under the Depot.
departments.hod_bio:
Brigadier General Buhari Michael Madaki is a member of 47 Regular Course of the Nigerian Defence Academy and Commissioning Course 001 of the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst. He is of the Infantry Corps.

He holds a Master of Arts in International Affairs and Strategic Studies from the Nigerian Defence Academy, a Master of Science in International Defence and Security from Cranfield University and an Executive Certificate in Public Leadership from the Harvard Kennedy School. He has attended all military courses commensurate with his rank, including the Higher Defence and Strategic Studies Course, National Defence College Abuja (September 2022 – September 2023); Higher Military Strategy and Management Course, Army War College Nigeria (April – December 2018); and Infantry Commanding Officer's Course, Nigerian Army School of Infantry Jaji (September – December 2016). He has completed the Advanced Airborne Course and is qualified as a jump master.

He has served in various capacities across instruction, staff, command and diplomacy, including: Instructor, Airborne Wing, Nigerian Army School of Infantry (2005 – 2008); Company Commander, 4 Guards Battalion (2008 – 2010); Military Assistant to the Commandant, Nigerian Defence Academy (2011 – 2012); Acting Commanding Officer, 72 Special Forces Battalion (2013 – 2014); Directing Staff, BMATT Junior Command and Staff Course, Canada (2007); Directing Staff, Armed Forces Command and Staff College Jaji (2016 – 2018); Defence Attaché to Mali with concurrent accreditation to Senegal, The Gambia, Mauritania, Cape Verde and Western Sahara (2019 – 2022); Directing Staff, National Defence College Abuja (2024 – 2025); and Commandant, Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo (current).

His awards and decorations include the Overseas Sword of Honour from the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst, the United Nations Mission in Liberia Medal, Nigeria Centenary and Golden Jubilee Medals, General Operations and Foreign Training Assistance Medals, and the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst and Command Medals. His hobbies include jogging, watching movies and wildlife.

members.name: Comdt Depot Na Osogbo
members.bio:
COMMANDANT – BRIGADIER GENERAL BUHARI MICHAEL MADAKI
Brigadier General Buhari Michael Madaki is a member of 47 Regular Course of the Nigerian Defence Academy and Commissioning Course 001 of the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst. He is of the Infantry Corps and has attended all military courses commensurate to his rank in the Infantry corps as well as several professional courses during his career. Some of these include the Higher Defence and Strategic Studies Course at the National Defence College Abuja (September 2022 to September 2023, Higher Military Strategy and Management Course at the Army War College Nigeria (April to December 2018 and Infantry Commanding Officer’s Course at the Nigerian Army School of Infantry Jaji (September to December 2016). He is a holder of master’s degrees in International Affairs and Strategic Studies from the Nigerian Defence Academy as well as International Defence and Security from Cranfield University.
Madaki has served in various capacities cutting across instruction, staff, command and diplomacy. Some of these include instructor at the Airborne Wing of the Nigerian Army School of Infantry (2005 to 2008) and Company Commander 4 Guards Battalion (2008 to 2010). He was Military Assistant to the Commandant of the Nigerian Defence Academy (2011 to 2012) and Acting Commanding Officer 72 Special Forces Battalion (2013 to 2014). He was privileged to serve as Directing Staff at the Military Training Assistance Programme Junior Command and Staff Course in Canada (2007), Armed Forces Command and Staff College Jaji (2016 to 2018) and National Defence College Abuja (2024 to 2025). He also served as Defence Attaché to Mali with concurrent accreditation to Senegal, The Gambia, Mauritania, Cape Verde and Western Sahara (2019 to 2022).
Madaki is the recipient of the Overseas Sword of Honour from the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst. His other awards and decorations include the United Nations Mission in Liberia medal, Nigeria Centenary and Golden Jubilee medals as well as General Operations and Foreign Training Assistance medals. Others are the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst and Command medals. Having attended the Advanced Airborne Course and qualified as a jump master, Buhari is passionate about the Airborne and strives to remain physically and mentally fit always. He is an alumnus of the Harvard Kennedy School and holder of an executive certificate in public leadership. He is a wildlife enthusiast and enjoys jogging and watching movies. His philosophy is hinged on the principle of goodwill and leadership through Service. He is married with children.
Madaki was appointed as pioneer Commandant of Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo on 5 June 2025.

members.name: Cpl Kamilu O
members.bio:
13NA/70/3976 Cpl Kamilu Olonade is a soldier of the Infantry Corps Centre (ICC) of the Nigerian Army. He was born on 10 October 1990 in Modakeke, Ife East Local Government Area of Osun State, Nigeria. He is married and a practising Muslim. He currently resides at Depot Nigerian Army Barracks, Osogbo, Osun State. Cpl Olonade began his educational journey at Modern Age Nursery and Primary School from 1996 - 2004, where he obtained his First School Leaving Certificate (FSLC). He subsequently attended Royal Comprehensive High School, Modakeke from 2004 - 2010, where he obtained his Senior Secondary School Certificate Examination (SSCE).
He was enlisted into the Nigerian Army as a member of the 70 Regular Recruits Intake (70 RRI). Following his initial military training at Depot Nigerian Army, Zaria, he was posted to 103 Battalion Nigerian Army, Awkunanaw, Enugu State, on 13 December 2013, where he served diligently from 2013 - 2025. Throughout his military career, Cpl Kamilu has attended several professional military courses, including the Basic Counter-Terrorism Course, Clerk General Duty Course B3, Clerk General Duty Course B2, and the Advanced Clerk Course B1. These courses have enhanced his professional competence in administration, personnel management and military operations. Presently, He is deployed as a Clerk/Computer Operator at the Headquarters Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo, where he performs administrative and information management duties.
Cpl Olonade has participated in several military operations and exercises, including Operation HADIN KAI in the North-East Theatre of Operations, Exercise CROCODILE SMILE, and Exercise PYTHON DANCE in the South-East, as well as various internal security operations across the country. Through these deployments, he has acquired extensive experience in counter-insurgency operations, troop administration, and leadership.
In recognition of his dedication, commitment, and exemplary service, he has received several awards and commendations, including the Chief Panther Award for Best Headquarters Soldier and the Operation HADIN KAI Medal, among others. He possesses excellent computer skills and is proficient in the use of Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel, Microsoft PowerPoint, and various internet applications. His hobbies include reading, sports, research, and community service.
Cpl Kamilu Olonade remains committed to the core values of the Nigerian Army and continues to contribute professionally and diligently to the service of the nation.

members.bio:

Lt Col Kester Ekwueme Ebie was born on 11 May 1985 in Agbor and hails from Ika North East Local Government Area of Delta State. He had his primary education at Command Children School, Ikeja and attended Command Secondary School, Jos, Plateau State, for his secondary education. He has attended several military courses, including the Young Officers' Course (Artillery), Troop Commanders' Course, Regimental Officers' Gunnery Course, Battery Commanders' Course and Regimental Command Course (Artillery). Others are Regimental Intelligence and Security Officers' Course, Basic French Course, Civil-Military Cooperation Staff Officers' Course and Military Observers' Course, as well as the Junior and Senior Staff Officers' Courses at the Armed Forces Command and Staff College, Nigeria.
Throughout his career, Lt Col Ebie has served in various command, staff and instructional appointments. These include Troop Commander, Adjutant and Platoon Commander in the United Nations Hybrid Operation in Darfur, Staff Officer at Sector 1 Headquarters UNAMID, Battery Commander at 375 Artillery Regiment Baga, State Recruitment Officer in Ogun State, Staff Officer Grade 2 Administration at HQs Depot NA Zaria, and Assistant Director Force Generation at AHQ Department of Army Operations as well as Staff Officer Grade 1 CIMIC Cell at the Multinational Joint Task Force Headquarters, N'Djamena. He is currently serving as Staff Officer Grade 1 Administration at Depot NA Osogbo and also doubles as Acting Chief of Staff.
He has participated in several operations, including OPLS II, OPLD, OPHK and OPRK II. Others are OPDP I and II and OP Mesa and other internal security operations. He equally served 2 tours of duty in the United Nations Hybrid Operation in Darfur in 2009 and 2012. Lieutenant Colonel Ebie is married to Mrs. Vivian Ogochukwu Ebie, and they are blessed with 3 children. His hobbies include watching movies, playing badminton, and travelling.

departments.name: Islamic Affairs
departments.brief:
The Depot NA Islamic Affairs, Osogbo, provides moral and spiritual guidance to Muslim personnel, their families and recruits in line with the mandate of the Directorate of Islamic Affairs, Nigerian Army.

The department's mandates include advising the Depot Commandant on Islamic matters; leading Friday prayers and the five daily prayers; providing spiritual guidance and counselling to officers, soldiers, families and recruits; officiating naming ceremonies, wedding ceremonies and funeral prayers; and conducting spiritual healing where required.

departments.name: Light Aid Detachment
departments.brief:
The Light Aid Detachment (LAD) was established in June 2025 to provide equipment support to Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. Its statutory responsibilities include the maintenance of electrical, mechanical, optical and electro-medical equipment across the Depot, and providing technical advice to the Commandant on Electrical and Mechanical Engineers matters. The detachment's current manpower strength is 1 x 9.

Since its establishment, the LAD has accomplished the following: routine servicing of vehicles and power generating sets; fabrication of 29 falling plates; repair of 2 unserviceable Turismo buses; refurbishment of the Review Officer's inspection vehicle used for 98 RRI POP; reinforcement and repair of Depot tentages; recovery duties; and retrofitting of a Toyota Hilux vehicle for the Osun State NYSC Secretariat.

He obtained a Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics from the University of Nigeria Nsukka in 2008 and served with the National Youth Service Corps at Government Secondary School Serti Barwa, Gashaka LGA, Taraba State (2009 – 2010).

He was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Electrical and Mechanical Engineers Corps on 25 May 2013 as a member of Short Service Combatant Course 41.

He has served in the following appointments: Pupilage, Command Workshop Mogadishu Cantonment Abuja (2013 – 2015); Acting Vehicle Platoon Commander, 35 Artillery Brigade Workshop Alamala Abeokuta (2016 – 2018); Vehicle Platoon Commander, 1041 Brigade Workshop Ikeja Cantonment (Jan 2016 – Oct 2018); OC Repair Company, Nigerian Army Special Retrofitting and Repair Workshop Ojo Cantonment Lagos (Oct 2018 – Feb 2022); Acting CO Brigade Workshop, 25 Task Force Brigade Damboa (2022); Acting Production Officer, 1033 Brigade Workshop Gibson Jalo Cantonment Yola (Nov 2022 – Jan 2024); Acting Production Officer, 32 Artillery Brigade Workshop Owena Cantonment Akure (Mar 2024); and Pioneer Officer Commanding, Light Aid Detachment, Depot NA Osogbo (current).

Courses attended include Basic Counter Terrorism Course Kontagora; EME Young Officers Course NASEME Auchi; Network Systems and Object Tracking Course DADP Lagos; Officers Armament and Instrument Course NASEME Auchi; Junior Staff Course AFCSC Jaji; Database Management System Course DADP Lagos; and COREN Annual Seminars. He is a registered member of the Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria (COREN) and a Fellow of the Institute of Security Nigeria.

His operational experience includes: Op MESA (Sagamu and Ogere, 9 Brigade AOR); Op HARBIN KUNAMA II, Southern Kaduna; Op HADIN KAI as Acting CO Brigade Workshop, 25 TF Bde Damboa; and Op SAFE CONDUCT (2023).

departments.name: Legal Department
departments.brief: The Legal Department provides legal advisory, review and instructional services to Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. Its functions include drafting and reviewing contracts to protect the interests of the Depot; identifying legal risks and ensuring compliance with relevant laws and policies; conducting legal reviews of Military Police Investigation Reports, Boards of Inquiry and Regimental Inquiries; and delivering lectures on Basic Military Law, the Law of Armed Conflict, Rules of Engagement, the status of Prisoners of War and Peace Support Operations, in collaboration with the Provost Company.
departments.hod_bio:
Captain E.F. Collins hails from Sabatoru Town in Nembe Local Government Area of Bayelsa State. He was born into the family of Chief Collins Ekine Eyeke and Mrs Stella Ekine Collins and is the first of three children.

He began his primary education at Township Primary School, Moscow Road, Port Harcourt in 1990 and attended Baptist High School Borokiri, Port Harcourt for his secondary education (SSCE 2002). He obtained a Bachelor of Laws from Niger Delta University, Bayelsa State (2010) and was called to the Nigerian Bar as a Barrister and Solicitor of the Supreme Court of Nigeria at the Nigerian Law School, Lagos Campus, on 25 November 2014.

He was commissioned into the Nigerian Army as a Lieutenant on 3 June 2017 as a member of Direct Short Service Course 23, and was initially posted to 82 Division Legal Services. He was converted from Direct Short Service Commission to Direct Regular Commission in August 2020.

He has served in the following appointments: Legal Officer, 82 Division Legal Services Enugu; Legal Officer, HQ Sector 3 Op HADIN KAI Monguno; Legal Officer, 31 Artillery Brigade Minna; Legal Officer, HQ Directorate of Legal Services Army Abuja; Prosecutor, HQ 7 Division General Court Martial; Judge Advocate, 1 Division General Court Martial; and Acting Assistant Director Legal, Depot NA Osogbo (18 February 2026 – current).

His operational experience includes Operation LAFIYA DOLE / HADIN KAI (North East), Operation DOMINION STORM, Operation WHIRL PUNCH and Operation MESA (all North Central Nigeria).

members.name: Capt Ef Collins
members.bio:
Capt EF Collins is an indigene of Sabatoru Town in Nembe Local Government Area of Bayelsa State. He was born to the family of Chief Collins Ekine Eyeke and Mrs Stella Ekine Collins. He is the first child of 3 children. He started his primary education in 1990 at Township Primary School, Moscow Road, Port Harcourt. On completion of his primary education, He was admitted into Baptist High School Borokiri Port Harcourt for his secondary education. He obtained his Senior Secondary School Certificate of Education in 2002. Thereafter, he gained admission into Niger Delta University, Bayelsa State where he obtained a Bachelor of Laws in 2010 and proceeded to the Nigerian Law School, Lagos Campus. He was called to the bar as a Barrister and Solicitor of the Supreme Court of Nigeria on November 25 2014.
Capt Collins was commissioned Lieutenant having successfully completed his cadet training on 3 June 2017 as a member of the Direct Short Service Course 23. He was subsequently posted to 82 Division Legal Services as his mother unit. In August 2020, he was converted from Direct Short Service Commission to Direct Regular Commission. In the officer’s career that is approaching a decade of meritorious service, he has participated in several operations, served in different units and attended military courses commensurate to his rank. These are highlighted in the succeeding paragraphs.
OPERATIONAL EXPERIENCE
Capt Collins has participated in several miliary operations including Operation Lafiya Dole which was later changed to Operation Hadin Kai in North East, Nigeria. Others are Operations Dominion Storm, Whirl Punch and Mesa all in North Central, Nigeria.
POSTING HISTORY
Capt Collins after his commission, has served in several units and formations including 82 Division Legal Services Enugu, HQ Sector 3 Op Hadin Kai Monguno. Others are 31 Artillery Brigade Minna and HQ DLS (A) Abuja. While serving in these units, he was sometimes redeployed to other formations for court martial duties. This led to his appointment as a prosecutor at HQ 7 Division General Court Martial and also as Judge Advocate 1 Division General Court Marial. He currently the Acting Assistant Director, Legal of Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo.
COURSES ATTENDED
On commission as a Lieutenant, the officer attended Leadership and Regimentation Course from July to September 2017 at 20 Model Bn, Serti Baruwa, Taraba State. On completion of the course at 20 Model Bn, he attended Judge Advocate General Basic Course from January to April 2019 at Nigerian Army School of Legal Services, Basawa, Kaduna State. Thereafter, he attended Junior Leadership and Management Course at Nigerian Army Resource Centre, Asokoro, Abuja. Additionally, Capt Collins has attended Junior Leadership and Staff Officers Course at the Nigerian Army College of Logistics and Management (NACOLM), Lagos. He also been privileged to attend a couple of courses, seminars and workshops locally and abroad that have in no small measure broadened his knowledge about law and the army at large.
Lastly, the officer gained promotion to the rank of Captain on 3 June 2022. In the course of his career in the NA, he has mentored several young officers, soldiers and civilians that he has crossed path with.  His hobbies include playing chess, research, travelling and engaging in positive educative conversations. He also occasionally enjoys playing table tennis.

members.name: Sgt Bissan - Legal Services
members.bio:
Sergeant Bissan Daniel was born on 20/12/1979 in the family of Mr and Mrs Patrick Bissan. He attended LEA primary school Marsa from 1985 to 1990. He later proceeded to Government Secondary school Ungwa Rimi from 1991 to 1996 all in Zango Kataf Local Government Area of Kaduna state. He was enlisted into Nigerian Army in 1997 as a member of 45 RRI. Upon the competition of his basic military training at Depot Nigerian Army Zaria, he was posted to the Infantry Corps where he served for four years. He was later admitted to study Diploma in law from 2001 to 2003.
The soldier also went ahead to study Advance Diploma in Law Security and Conflict Resolution from 2004 to 2005. Sergeant Bissan decided to further his education by pursuing a Bsc in Social work and Administration from 2008 to 2012. He subsequently obtained an LLB Degree between 2009 and 2014 from the University of Jos. He later proceeded to the Nigerian Law school where he obtained BL and was called to the Nigerian Bar as a Barrister and solicitor of Supreme Court of Nigeria.
After the call to bar of Sergeant Bissan, he was posted to the Directorate of Legal Services (Army) as a prosecutor in 1 Division Nigerian Army General Court Martial. He was thereafter converted as a stand-bye defence counsel where he defended both officers and soldiers in the court. Additionally, the soldier in collaboration with external solicitors defended Nigerian Army in civil courts. Sergeant Bissan is currently serving in Depot Nigerian Army Legal Services Osogbo as an instructor of military law. He also carries out other military duties and assignments as situation demands.

departments.name: Medical Centre
departments.brief: The Medical Centre provides primary and secondary healthcare services to all military personnel and their dependants within Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. The centre conducts routine medical examinations for recruits on admission and throughout training, manages sick parades, emergency care and medical evacuations. It is staffed by trained military medical officers and support personnel committed to maintaining the health and fitness of the Depot community.
departments.hod_bio:
Major (Dr.) Jonathan Anietie Edimek is a military medical officer and healthcare administrator with over 26 years of service in the Nigerian Army and more than 14 years of clinical practice.

He commenced his military career in October 1999 following graduation from the Nigerian Military School Zaria. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Microbiology from the University of Uyo (2005), a Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) from the University of Calabar (2011), a Master of Business Administration from Ahmadu Bello University Zaria (2023) and a Master of Public Health from the same institution. He completed his Housemanship at the University of Uyo Teaching Hospital in 2012 and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Medical Corps as a member of Direct Short Service Course 21 in 2014.

He has served in a broad range of operational, regimental, national and international appointments including: Military Hospital Lagos; 242 Battalion Konduga; 5 Brigade Konduga; Military Hospital Sokoto; 212 Battalion Konduga during Operations DEEP PUNCH II and RESCUE FINALE; 243 Battalion Badagry; 15 Field Engineer Regiment Badagry; 12 Field Engineer Regiment Ijebu-Ode; 1 Battalion Birnin Kebbi; Defence Space Administration Clinic Abuja; and Acting Commanding Officer, Depot NA Medical Centre Osogbo (current).

His international service includes the ECOWAS Mission in The Gambia (ECOMIG) and the United Nations-African Union Mission in Darfur (UNAMID). He also served as Team Lead for the Defence Headquarters COVID-19 North-West Response. His research interests cover public health, disease prevention, healthcare access and health systems strengthening.

members.name: Ahmad Introduction-1
members.bio:
A BRIEF INTRODUCTION OF MY LIFE
INTRODUCTION
I was born on Sunday 25th of January 1996 into the family of Mr. and Mrs Ahmad Alhaji Ibrahim. I am the fifth child of eleven children. I hail from Tilden Fulani community in Toro Local Government Area of Bauchi state. My parents are both civil servants who raised my siblings and I to love each other and our neighbors as we love ourselves.
I am a Muslim by faith. I enjoy watching movies, reciting the Quran, playing football and watching movies. I support Real Madrid football club and I enjoy eating various Nigerian cuisine.
EARLY CHILDHOOD AND EDUCATION
I spent my early childhood in Tilde where I attended Tilden Fulani Primary School to earn my first school leaving certificate between 2001 and 2007. Thereafter, I proceeded to Government Day junior Secondary School Tilden Fulani I obtained my Junior WAEC Certificate. I preceded Government Day Senior Secondary School in the same town for my senior secondary education for 3 years. Upon successfully completing my secondary school education I was offered admission into the Bauchi State University to study Public Health Technology. I went for my SIWES at Abubakar Tafawa Balewa University Teaching Hospital Bauchi under the community Medicine department I was posted to several units during my SIWES which includes the TB dots Center, immunization, child welfare clinic we were also posted to Bauchi state Environmental Protection Agency (BASEPA) And Bauchi state water Board treatment Plant. The SIWES lasted for 6 months after a very challenging 4 years I graduated with a BSc. Degree.
My career as a public health professional after my one year National service at Jigawa State as a class teacher at Government Day Girls Arabic School Kazaure. After my NYSC I have worked with the INGOs like UNICEF in Polio Eradication Program, USAID as a public health officer water sanitation and hygiene promotion.
I was fortunate to gain experience at different health care facilities including the Bauchi state primary healthcare Development Agency and the my town Primary Healthcare Center (PHC), participated fully in outbreaks of Coronavirus (COVID-19) response team, SMC, NTDs, Polio Eradication programs. I also lectured in Al-iklass College of health Technology under community health department for 2 years and worked as a public health nurse at Fabil pharmaceuticals limited. I also obtained different professional certificates with advanced health and safety management (HSE) level 3.
I am an Associate member of the African Institute of Public Health Professional (AIPHP) and also a Member of the British Society for Antimicrobial Chemotherapy (BSACT).
MILITARY CAREER
I applied and got shortlisted for military training as a member of Direct Short Service Course 27 Army.  My military training began on 15 Apr 23 at the Nigerian Defense Academy (NDA) and I was commissioned Lieutenant on 30 Sep 23 and posted to the Nigerian Army Medical Corps.
Over the course of my military career I have attended Leadership and Regimentation Course Nguru, Yobe State. I have served at 31 Brigade Medical center Minna, Niger State and currently I am posted to DEPOT NA Oshogbo Medical Center.
F AHMAD
Lt
Jul 25

members.name: Capt A Hassan
members.bio:
Abdullahi Hassan was born on 13 May 92 into the family of Mr and Mrs Hassan Abubakar. He hails from Gwarzo Local Government Area of Kano State. He attended Dakwara Primary School from 1997 to 2003. Thereafter, he proceeded to Science Secondary School, Dawakin Kudu, from 2006 to 2009, where he obtained his Senior Secondary School Certificate Examination (SSCE). He later gained admission into Bayero University Kano, Kano State, where he studied from 2011 to 2016 and graduated with a Bachelor degree in Physiotherapy.
Capt Hassan was commissioned into the Nigerian Army on 25 Jul 20 as a member of Direct Short Service Course (DSSC) 25 and was posted to the Nigerian Army Medical Corps. Throughout his military career, he has served in several units, including HQ NAMC Lagos, 3 DMSH Jos, HQ NABFC Falgore, 68 NARHY Lagos, 44 NARHK Kaduna. He is currently serving at Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo as a senior physiotherapist. He has attended several military courses commensurate to his rank. Notable among these are the Leadership and Regimentation Course at Serti Baruwa (2020), Young Officers Course Medical at Ojo (2021), Legal Aspect of Internal Security Operations Course at NASLS Basawa (2023), the Young Officers Course (Infantry) at Jaji (2024) and Functional Arabic Course at Ilorin (2025).
Beyond his military duties, Capt Hassan enjoys reading, physical exercises, travelling, and watching American series. He is happily married and the union is blessed with a daughter.

members.name: Lt Mu Anikputa
members.bio: Lt Maryjuliet Ukamaka Anikputa was born on 8 July 1994 in Onitsha, Anambra State, into the family of Mr and Mrs Christopher Anikputa. She hails from Ezeagu Local Government Area of Enugu State. She attended St. Charles Lwanga International School for both her primary and secondary education, where she obtained her First School Leaving Certificate in 2002 and her Junior and Senior Secondary School Certificates in 2008. Thereafter, she proceeded to the University of Maiduguri College of Nursing in 2014, where she studied Nursing and subsequently specialized in Psychiatric and Mental Health Nursing in 2017. Lt Anikputa was later admitted into the prestigious Nigerian Defence Academy and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army on 26 February 2022 as a member of the 47 Regular Combatant Course with the rank of Second Lieutenant. Upon commissioning, she was posted to the Nigerian Army Medical Corps. Throughout her military career, she has served in several units, including 68 Nigerian Army Reference Hospital from 4 July 2022 to 6 June 2025 and Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo, where she is currently serving as the Acting Chief Nursing Officer. She has attended several military courses commensurate with her rank and responsibilities. Thereafter, he then proceeded to Government Day junior Secondary School Tilden Fulani where obtained his Junior WAEC Certificate. He also preceded to Government Day Senior Secondary School. Upon successful completion his secondary school education he was offered admission into the Bauchi State University to study Public Health Technology. He went for his SIWES at Abubakar Tafawa Balewa University Teaching Hospital Bauchi under the community Medicine department he was posted to several units during his SIWES which includes the TB dots Center, immunization, child welfare clinic we were also posted to Bauchi state Environmental Protection Agency (BASEPA) And Bauchi state water Board treatment Plant. The SIWES lasted for 6 months after a very challenging 4 years he graduated with a BSc. Degree. His career as a public health professional after his one-year National service at Jigawa State as a class teacher at Government Day Girls Arabic School Kazaure. After his NYSC, he worked with the INGOs like UNICEF in Polio Eradication Program, USAID as a public health officer water sanitation and hygiene promotion.
Faisal was fortunate to gain experience at different health care facilities, including the Bauchi State Primary Healthcare Development Agency and his town's Primary Healthcare Center (PHC). members.name: Biography Maj Dr Ja Edimek And Depot Na Os
members.bio:
MAJOR (DR) JONATHAN ANIETIE EDIMEK PJLSC, MBBS, MBA, MPH Bsc MCB ,Ag CO, Depot Nigerian Army Medical Centre, Osogbo
Maj (Dr) Jonathan Anietie Edimek is military medical officer, healthcare administrator, public health researcher with over 26 years of meritorious service in the NA and more than 14 years of clinical medical practice, he has consistently demonstrated exceptional commitment to health care. Maj Edimek commenced his military career in October 1999 after passing out of the prestigious NMS zaria, and subsequently earned his Bachelor of science in Microbiology and then a Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) degree from the University of Uyo and University of Calabar in 2005 and 2011 respectively. He completed his Housemanship at the University of Uyo Teaching Hospital in 2012 before joining the Nigerian Army Medical Corps as a member of DSSC 21 in 2014. He further enhanced his professional competence by obtaining an MBA from Ahmadu Bello University, Zaria, in 2023 and a Masters in Public Health (MPH) at the same institution.
Throughout his military career, Maj Edimek has served in numerous operational, regimental, national, and international appointments. He served with the ECOWAS Mission in The Gambia (ECOMIG), the United Nations-African Union Mission in Darfur (UNAMID). He served as Team Lead for the Defence Headquarters COVID-19 North-West Response. Major Edimek has also held appointments at the Military Hospital Lagos (MHL), 242 Bn Konduga, 5 bde Konduga, Military Hospital Sokoto, 212Bn Konduga during Operations DEEP PUNCH II and RESCUE FINALE, 243 Bn Badagry, 15 Field Engineer Regiment badagry, 12 FER Ijebuode,1 Bn Birnin Kebbi, Defence Space Administration Clinic Abuja, and currently serves as the Ag CO, Depot Nigerian Army Medical Centre, Osogbo.
As the pioneer CO of the newly established Depot NA Med Cen, Osogbo, Major Edimek has overseen a remarkable transformation of the facility from its formative stage into a functional and expanding healthcare institution supporting recruit training and military healthcare delivery . Beyond clinical practice and military leadership, Major Edimek is an accomplished researcher whose work focuses on public health, disease prevention, healthcare access, and health systems strengthening. His notable research includes studies on malaria prevention practices in urban and rural communities of Abuja, the impact of cashless policy implementation in Nigeria, and collaborative research in obstetrics and gynaecology and cancer epidemiology.
He is a member of several professional bodies, including the Nigerian Medical Association (NMA), the Medical and Dental Council of Nigeria (MDCN), and the Nigerian Army Resource Centre. He has also completed numerous professional and military courses in Tactical Combat Casualty Care, Advanced Cardiac Life Support, Basic Life Support, Military Public Health, Peace Support Operations, HIV/AIDS Programming, Maternal and Child Health, military leadership and most recently, he is certified in Global leadership and management in Health and Project management in Global Health from the University of Washington.  Hes hobbies are reading, drawing/painting, singing and helping the sick. He is happily married with 2 children.
DEPOT NIGERIAN ARMY MEDICAL CENTRE, OSOGBO
INTRODUCTION
The Depot NA Med Cen, Osogbo, serves as the primary healthcare institution of Depot NA and is responsible for providing comprehensive medical support to recruits, military personnel, their families, civilian staff, and other authorized beneficiaries. Since its establishment, the Med Cene has remained committed to preserving health, enhancing operational readiness, and supporting the training mission of the Depot.
HUMBLE BEGINNINGS
The Medical Centre commenced operations alongside the establishment of Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo, with a modest workforce comprising only the pioneer Commanding Officer, Major (Dr) Jonathan Anietie Edimek, and eight military personnel. Despite limited manpower, infrastructure, and resources, the Medical Centre successfully provided essential healthcare services to the growing Depot community while laying the foundation for future expansion.
Through strategic planning, effective leadership, and support from Headquarters Depot Nigerian Army, the Medical Centre has witnessed remarkable growth in manpower, infrastructure, and service delivery. Today, the Medical Centre boasts a workforce of 6 Officers, 29 Soldierl, 11 Civilian Staff, 15 National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) Members. In addition, the facility serves as a training institution for healthcare professionals and currently accommodates: 22 Students Industrial Work Experience Scheme (SIWES) trainees
DEPARTMENTS AND SERVICES
The Med Cen currently operates through several functional departments, including:
Medical Outpatient Department (MOPD). Provides consultation, diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up care for patients.
Nursing Services Department. Offers inpatient care, medication administration, patient monitoring, health education, and emergency nursing services.
Laboratory Department. Provides diagnostic laboratory investigations essential for disease diagnosis, treatment, surveillance, and medical screening.
Radiology Department. Provides diagnostic imaging services including Digital X-Ray investigations to support clinical decision-making.
Pharmacy Department. Responsible for procurement, storage, dispensing, and monitoring of medications and medical consumables.
Public Health Department. Conducts disease prevention, health promotion, environmental sanitation, surveillance, immunization, and outbreak control activities.
Medical Records Department. Maintains patient records, hospital statistics, and supports the Electronic Medical Records (EMR) system.
Emergency and Observation Services. Provides immediate care and stabilization for medical emergencies arising within the Depot.
CORE FUNCTIONS
The Med Cen performs the following key functions:
Medical screening and assessment of recruits.
Provision of primary and secondary healthcare services.
Emergency medical response and stabilization.
Medical cover for military training activities and EX.
Disease prevention and public health interventions.
Occupational health services.
Health education and promotion.
Referral coordination with specialist and tertiary hospitals.
Medical administration and record management.
Clinical training and mentorship of corps members and students.
CONCLUSION
From its humble beginnings with one MO and 8 soldiers, the Depot NA Med Cen Oshogbo has grown into a vibrant healthcare institution providing quality medical services, training future healthcare professionals, and safeguarding the health of recruits, soldiers, and their families. Through dedication, innovation, and teamwork, the Medical Centre continues to contribute significantly to the operational effectiveness and readiness of Depot NA, Osogbo.

departments.name: Physical Training Wing
departments.brief:
The Physical Training (PT) Wing is responsible for planning, coordinating, supervising and executing all physical training activities for personnel and recruits at Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. It develops and sustains the physical, mental and psychological readiness of soldiers through structured programmes designed to enhance strength, endurance, agility, stamina, discipline, resilience and combat efficiency. The wing's current posted strength is 2 x 43 soldiers.

Training activities conducted by the wing include morning physical training, evening games, Bi-Annual Physical Efficiency Tests (BPET), endurance race training and competitions, obstacle training and competitions, novices boxing bouts, circuit training, sports week competitions, unarmed combat and self-defence training, and the EX ETI PARI Dash Back exercise.

Arogun began his educational journey at St. Anthony Roman Catholic Mission School, Akure, where he obtained his First School Leaving Certificate (FSLC) in 1999 after completing his primary education between 1993 and 1999. He subsequently attended St. Michael Catholic High School, Akure, from 2000 to 2006, where he earned his Senior Secondary School Certificate (SSCE).
Driven by a passion for physical education and sports development, he proceeded to Obafemi Awolowo University, Ile-Ife, Osun State, where he obtained a Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) degree in Physical and Health Education between 2012 and 2016. Following his graduation, he participated in the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) programme and served at the Nigerian Army School of Mechanical Engineering, Makurdi, Benue State, from 2017 to 2018.
He was commissioned into the Nigerian Army on 20 July 2019 as a member of Direct Short Service Course 24 under the Directorate of Army Physical Training. Through dedication, professionalism, and outstanding service, he was subsequently converted to a Direct Regular Commission (DRC) officer in 2022.
Throughout his military career, he has attended and successfully completed several professional and military courses aimed at enhancing his leadership, operational, and administrative capabilities. These include the Leadership and Regimentation Course at Serti Baruwa, the Basic Unarmed Combat Course in Zaria, the Mechanical Transport Officer’s Course in Benin City, the Young Officers’ Course (Physical Training) in Zaria, the Advanced Sports Management Course in Zaria, the Young Officers’ Course (Infantry) in Jaji, and the Advanced Physical Training Officers’ Course in Zaria.
Oluwakayode has served in various capacities within the Nigerian Army. His appointments have included Platoon Commander in the Directorate of Army Physical Training, Administrative Officer at the 81 Division Physical Training Group, The pioneer Physical Training Team Leader of Exercise RESTORE HOPE at the Nigerian Army Base Camp Kachia. Through these appointments, Staff Officer Physical Training at Nigerian Army School of Finance and Administration, Apapa and He is currently serving as a pioneer instructor at Depot Nigerian Army Oshogbo deployed to PT Wing. He has demonstrated strong leadership, administrative competence, and commitment to the physical development and operational effectiveness of military personnel.
In addition to his military responsibilities, he maintains active membership in professional organizations. He is a member of the Nigerian Army Resource Centre and an Associate Member of the National Association for Physical Health Education, Recreation, Sports and Dance (NAPHER-SD).
Beyond his professional commitments, Mathew enjoys participating in various sporting activities and reading, which contribute to his personal development and professional effectiveness.
Captain Oluwakayode Mathew Arogun continues to serve the Nigerian Army with dedication, discipline, and professionalism while contributing meaningfully to the advancement of physical training, leadership development, and military excellence.

departments.name: Quartermaster Department
departments.brief:
The Quartermaster (QM) Department is responsible for providing logistics and administrative support to Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. Established on 7 June 2025 alongside the Depot, it has grown from 1 officer to a current strength of 2 x 16 soldiers. The department is a key component of the Depot's organisational structure and is the primary logistics branch charged with the issuance and management of stores, arms and ammunition.

Its responsibilities include the receipt, supply, distribution, procurement and maintenance of stores, uniforms, boots, bedding, training equipment, stationery and other military materiel; setting up camps for field training exercises; allocating accommodation for recruits and personnel; and maintaining inventory records.

departments.name: Records Office
departments.brief: The Records Office maintains comprehensive records of all military personnel who have served in or passed through Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. It handles documentation related to enlistment, posting, promotions, discharges and service history, and plays a vital role in ensuring the accuracy and integrity of all personnel data within the Depot formation.
departments.hod_bio:
Lieutenant Colonel Idowu Kayode Oladipo was born on 1 April 1985 into the family of the late Mr and Mrs B.O. Oladipo. He hails from Okemesi in Ekiti-West Local Government Area of Ekiti State.

He attended Command Children's School and Command Day Secondary School, Ojo Military Cantonment, Lagos State for his primary and secondary education. He was admitted to the Nigerian Defence Academy, Kaduna as a member of 57 Regular Course and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Corps of Artillery. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Geography from the NDA and a Master of Arts in Leadership and Security Studies (in view).

Courses attended include Young Officers Course Artillery, Troop Commander Course, Regimental Officers' Gunnery Course, Battery Commander Course, and Junior and Senior Command and Staff Courses at the Armed Forces Command and Staff College.

He has served in the following appointments: Battery Commander/Second-in-Command, 375 Artillery Regiment Baga and Mallam Fatori (Borno State); State Records Officer, Bauchi State; OC Forward Operating Bases Zurmi, Tsafe, Talata Mafara and Gudu (North West Theatre); SO1 G7, HQ Nigerian Army Corps of Artillery; and Head, Records Office, Depot NA Osogbo (current).

His operational experience includes UNAMID Sudan, Operation RESTORE ORDER I and II, Operation BOYONA, Operation ZAMAN LAFIYA, Operation LAFIYA DOLE (North East, Borno State) and Operation HADIN DAJI (North West, Sokoto and Zamfara States).

He is married. His hobbies include reading, listening to music and playing table tennis.

He has participated in UNAMID Ops Sudan, OP Restore Order 1&2, OP BOYONA, OP Zaman Lafiya, OP Lafiya Dole in NE, Borno state and OPHD in NW, Sokoto and Zamfara States.

members.name: Lt Y Dinshiya
members.bio:
Lieutenant Yisarga Dinshiya (N/19130) was born on 27 March 1995 to the family of Mr. and Mrs. Christopher Dinshiya in Jalingo, Taraba State. He hails from Donga Local Government Area of Taraba State. He completed his primary education at New Edition Nursery and Primary School, Jalingo, in 2004, and subsequently proceeded to Federal Government College, Wukari, where he obtained his West African Senior School Certificate in 2010.
He earned a Bachelor of Science degree in Accounting from Nasarawa State University, Keffi, in 2017, and completed his National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) in Nasarawa State in April 2019.
Lieutenant Dinshiya reported to the Nigerian Defence Academy on 25 January 2020 as a member of Short Service Course 46 and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army as a Second Lieutenant on 10 October 2020. He has attended several military courses, which include Advanced Infantry Training (Exercise Restore Hope) at Kachia, Company Amphibious Course at Calabar, Young Officers’ Course (Infantry) at Jaji, and Mortar Platoon Commanders’ Course at Jaji.
He is currently serving as the Administrative Officer (Records Office) at Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo.
Lieutenant Dinshiya is happily married to Mrs. Martha Martins Dinshiya. His hobbies include reading, playing football, and listening to music.

departments.name: Recruits Centre
departments.brief: The Recruits Centre is responsible for receiving, processing and administering new recruits from the point of enlistment through their entire basic military training. The centre ensures that all recruits meet the physical, medical and administrative requirements for service in the Nigerian Army, and manages their welfare, discipline and documentation throughout the training period.
departments.hod_bio:
Lieutenant Colonel Emmanuel Manasseh Ukande is a member of Short Service Course 33 of the Nigerian Defence Academy and was commissioned into the Infantry Corps in 2003.

He has attended military courses commensurate with his rank including Junior Course, AFCSC Jaji (January – June 2015); Senior Course, AFCSC Jaji (January 2019 – August 2020); Commanding Officers Course (January – April 2026); Company Commanders Course (September – December 2015); Young Officers Course (February – May 2006); Special Operations Command Course, Quanzhou Military Academy, China (August 2016 – July 2017); Basic and Advanced Airborne Courses, Airborne Wing Jaji (February – March 2005 and April – June 2026); Protection of Civilians Course, Martin Luther Agwai Leadership and Peacekeeping Centre Jaji (July – August 2016); and United Nations Battalion Course, South Korea (November 2022).

He has served in various staff, command and instructional appointments including Adjutant and Operations Officer, 192 Battalion (2003 – 2005); Platoon Commander, UNMIL (2006) and UNAMID (2007); Instructor and Administrative Officer, Tactics Wing, Nigerian Army School of Infantry (May – August 2016); Acting Sector Commander, Operations RESTORE ORDER III, BOYONA and ZAMAN LAFIYA (July 2011 – May 2016); Training Officer, Nigerian Army Special Forces School Buni Yadi (September 2020 – May 2021); Commanding Officer, 2 Special Women Operations Battalion Abakaliki (May 2021 – July 2023); Commanding Officer, 196 Amphibious Battalion Baga, Op HADIN KAI / Multinational Joint Task Force (July 2023 – May 2025); and Acting Commander, Recruits Centre, Depot NA Osogbo (May 2025 – current).

His awards and decorations include the Meritorious Service Star, Force Service Star, Passed Staff Course, Nigeria Centenary and Golden Jubilee Medals, and General Operations and Command Medals. He is married with children. His interests include physical fitness and mentoring subordinates.

members.name: Capt A Ibrahim
members.bio:
Captain Aliyu Ibrahim was born on the 13th of April 1993  into the family of  Alh Ibrahim Waziri. He hails from Hawul Local Government Area of Borno State
He attended Kida primary school where he obtained his first school leaving certificate, and thereafter proceeded to Salvation International School where he obtained his junior and senior school certificate. Captain Ibrahim was commissioned into the Nigerian Army to the rank of Second Lieutenant on the 05 Oct 19 as a member of 66 Regular Combatant Course (66 RC) and was posted to the Infantry Corp Centre(ICC)..

members.name: Capt Km Surajo
members.bio:
Khalid Surajo Muhammad was born on October 7, 1994, into the family of Mr. and Mrs. Surajo Muhammad. He hails from Gusau Local Government Area, Zamfara State.
He began his academic journey at Kaduna Capital School, Kaduna, attending from 2000 to 2006. He then proceeded to Command Secondary School, Kaduna, where he studied from 2007 to 2013 and successfully obtained his WAEC certificate.
In 2014, Khalid achieved a lifelong ambition by gaining admission into the prestigious Nigerian Defence Academy (NDA) as a member of the 66 Regular Course. He graduated in 2020 and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army, then posted to the Infantry Corps, marking the start of a dedicated military career.
Over the course of his service, Capt Surajo has served in several units, including: 13 Brigade Garrison, Calabar; 27 Task Force Brigade, Buni Gari; 156 Task Force Battalion, Mainok; 154 Task Force Battalion, Ngamdu; and he is presently serving at Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo.
To enhance his professional competence, he has attended several courses commensurate with his rank. Notable among them are the Leadership and Regimentation Course at Serti Baruwa (2020), the Young Officers Course (Infantry) in Jaji (2022), and the Anti-Tank Platoon Commander Course in Jaji (2024).
Beyond the military, Capt Surajo enjoys reading, cooking, and track events sports. He is happily married and remains committed to serving his nation with honor and integrity.

members.name: Capt Muhammad_Adamu_Yerima_Biography
members.bio:
MY ADAMU BIOGRAPHY
Muhammad Adamu Yerima was born on 10 October 1991 into the family of Mr. and Mrs. Adamu. He hails from Y/Deba Local Government Area, Gombe State.
He began his primary education at Kamara Primary School, Gombe State, where he studied from 1999 to 2005. He proceeded to Government Day Secondary School, Bolari East, Gombe State, where he studied from 2006 to 2011 and obtained his Senior Secondary School Certificate Examination (SSCE). He later gained admission into the University of Maiduguri (UNIMAID) in 2012, where he obtained a Bachelor’s Degree in Islamic Studies in 2016.
Upon completion of his mandatory National Youth Service, he was admitted into the Nigerian Army as a member of Direct Short Service Course 25 in 2020 and was commissioned into the Directorate of Islamic Affairs (DOIA) in the same year. Over the course of his service, he has served in several units, including 241 Battalion, Nguru; 3 Division, Jos; and 245 Battalion, Ikom, Calabar. He is currently serving at Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo, as an Instructor.
To enhance his professional competence, he has attended several courses commensurate with his rank. These include the Leadership and Regimentation Course at 20 Model Battalion, Serti Baruwa (2020), the Young Officers Course (DOIA) (2021), the Young Officers Course (Infantry) at the Nigerian Army School of Infantry (NASI), Jaji, Kaduna (2022), and the Basic Arabic Course at NAISET, Ilorin (2024).
Beyond his military career, he enjoys reading, cooking, and track and field athletics. He is happily married and remains committed to his professional development and service in the Nigerian Army.

members.name: Capt Ok Agbo
members.bio:
Captain Obinna Kingsley Agbo (N/16473) pjsc was born on 2 December 1989 into the family of Mr. and Mrs. Dominic Agbo of Udenu Local Government Area, Enugu State. He was commissioned as a Second Lieutenant on 12 October 2016 as a member of the 63 Regular Course.
Captain Agbo began his early education at Apapa Primary School, Lagos State (1993–1999), and later obtained his secondary school certificate from Command Secondary School, Kaduna (1999–2005). To sharpen his military expertise, he has completed several key courses, including the Young Officer Course Armour, Young Officers Course Infantry , Basic Airborne Course, Armour Platoon Commander Course, and the British Military Advisory Training Team Course in Jaji, Kaduna State.
His operational experience includes fight against insurgency in northeastern Nigeria, serving in Operation Lafiya Dole, Borno State (2017–2022), and subsequently Operation Hadin Kai. Over the course of his career, he has served in different formations and held several appointments commensurate with his rank, including Administrative Officer at 3 Division Training School, Jos; Acting Officer Commanding (OC) Bravo Company, AHQ Garrison; Liaison Officer at Mess 1, Asokoro, Abuja; and presently as OC Delta Company, Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo.
Captain Agbo is married and blessed with three children. In his leisure time, he enjoys judo and swimming.

members.bio:
CAPTAIN VF UCHE’S BIOGRAPHY
Captain V.F. Uche was born on 9 July 1996 to the family of Engr. Uche Eke Kalu and Mrs. Grace Uche. He hails from Arochukwu Local Government Area of Abia State, Nigeria.
The officer obtained his First School Leaving Certificate from Assemblies of God Nursery and Primary School, Kano, in 2006. He subsequently proceeded to Evangel Academy, Kubwa, Federal Capital Territory (FCT), where he obtained his Junior Secondary School Certificate. He later attended Government Science Secondary School, Pyakasa, FCT, where he earned his Senior Secondary School Certificate in 2012.
In September 2015, he was admitted into the prestigious Nigerian Defence Academy (NDA) as a member of the 67 Regular Course. Upon successful completion of his military and academic training, he was commissioned into the Nigerian Army as a Second Lieutenant on 10 October 2020 and was subsequently posted to the Nigerian Army Corps of Supply and Transport (NACST). Captain Uche holds a Bachelor of Engineering (B.Eng.) degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering from the Nigerian Defence Academy.
To enhance his professional competence and leadership capabilities, the officer has attended several military courses, including:
Leadership and Regimentation Course, 20 Model Battalion, Serti-Baruwa (2021).
Infantry Young Officers’ Course, Nigerian Army School of Infantry (NASI), Jaji (2021).
Basic French Language Course, Nigerian Army Language Institute (NALI), Ovim (2022).
Anti-Piracy Course, Martin Luther Agwai International Leadership and Peacekeeping Centre (MLAILPKC), Jaji (2022).
Supply and Transport Young Officers’ Course, Nigerian Army School of Supply and Transport (NASST), Benin (2022).
Catering Officers’ Course, Nigerian Army School of Supply and Transport (NASST), Benin (2023).
The officer served with 70 Command Supply and Transport, Apapa, Lagos, from 2020 to 2022. He was subsequently posted to 77 Supply and Transport Brigade, where he served from December 2022 to June 2025. In June 2025, he was posted to Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo, where he currently serves as an Instructor.
Captain V.F. Uche is single. In his leisure time, he enjoys reading and engaging in intellectual discussions as well as exploring innovative ideas that contribute to personal development and professional excellence.
