members.name: Capt S Awanyi Biography
members.bio:
RESUME OF CAPTAIN SYLVANUS ANWAYI
Captain Sylvanus Anwayi was born in Kaduna State to the family of the late Mr. and Mrs. Dominic Anwayi and hails from Kaura Local Government Area of Kaduna State. He obtained his First School Leaving Certificate from LGA Primary School, Mahuta, before proceeding to Barewa College, Zaria, where he completed his secondary education. He later gained admission into Ahmadu Bello University, Zaria, and graduated in 2009 with a Bachelor’s degree in Urban and Regional Planning.
Capt Anwayi was commissioned into the Nigerian Army as a Second Lieutenant on 12 September 2015 as a member of Short Service Combatant Course 43. He was initially posted to the Armoured Corps before converting to the Infantry Corps in 2016. Throughout his military career, he has served in several units, including 244 Battalion, 3 Battalion (M), 3 Battalion (R), 7 Guards Battalion, and the Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo, where he is currently serving. He has held various appointments such as Intelligence Officer, Adjutant, Operations Officer, Quartermaster, Platoon Commander, and Acting Officer Commanding.
During his service, Capt Anwayi has participated in several military operations, including Operation Burst, Operation Lafiya Dole, and Operation Amni Fakad. He has also attended a number of professional military courses, notably the Infantry Young Officers Course, Basic Range Management Course, Anti-Tank Guided Weapon Course, and Train-the-Trainer Course at the Nigerian Army School of Infantry as well as Junior Staff Course at The Armed Forces Command and Staff College Jaji. He is married and blessed with a son. Outside his professional duties, he enjoys listening to music, travelling, meditating, and playing snooker.

members.name: Lt Ne_Eshiet[1]
members.bio:
Lieutenant Ndifreke Essien Eshiet was born on 7 June, 1992 in Calabar,  he received his early education at Evangel Nursery and Primary School,Uyo, Akwa Ibom State before proceeding to Hope Academy, Ikot Ekpe for his secondary education. Lieutenant Eshiet gained admission into the University of Calabar,Calabar to study Microbiology and graduated in 2015. He was posted to Kebbi State for the compulsory National Youth Service Corps in 2017 through 2018. Due to passion to serve his nation, he joined the Army as a member of Short Service Combatant  Course 45 after 9 months of rigorous military training at the Nigerian Defence Academy Kaduna and was subsequently commissioned into the rank of Second Lieutenant in October 2019.
Throughout his career, Lieutenant Eshiet has served in various units which include 198 Special Forces Battalion, Borno State as Platoon Commander and 343 Artillery Regiment, Rivers State as Troop Commander after which he was appointed Adjutant. Lieutenant Eshiet partook in various military Operations while in the theatre and his contributions to military operations and unit administration was commendable. He began serving at Depot Nigerian Army, Osogbo from June 2025 as one of the pioneer instructors responsible for the training and development of recruits.
Lieutenant Eshiet has held several appointments while at Depot, including Acting Second-In-Command (2IC) Golf Training Company, Acting Officer Commanding Golf Training Company, Acting 2IC India Training Company, Acting 2IC Hotel Training Company and currently the Acting Administrative Officer Recruits Center. Beyond his command responsibilities, he oversees the administration of recruits during the period of training in Depot.
He also plays a vital role by ensuring that recruits adhere strictly to the Dos and Don’ts of the Depot and also carry out disciplinary measures on defaulters. Lieutenant Eshiet is dedicated to duty and also committed to the development of the next generation of soldiers through exemplary and effective leadership.

members.name: Lt Ob Adefemi
members.bio:
Lieutenant Olawale Bamidele Adefemi (N/18871) was born in Kano State on 4 Oct 92 into the family of Late Sqn Ldr MO Adefemi (Rtd) and Late Chief (Mrs) Bolanle Aduke Adefemi. He hails from Ibadan South-West Local Government Area of Oyo State. He attended Epic Academy, Naibawa, Kano State where he obtained his First School Leaving Certificate. He then proceeded to Emphatic Neo-Classic College, Kano State for his secondary school education. He attended Oyo State College of Agriculture Igboora, Oyo State where he obtained his National Diploma in Agricultural and Bio-Environmental Engineering and bagged his Higher National Diploma (HND) Certificate in Agricultural Engineering from Federal College of Agriculture, Ibadan, Oyo State.
Upon completion of his HND, he was mobilized as a National Youth Service Corps member in year 2015 and was subsequently posted to Jigawa State. He was later shortlisted into the Nigerian Army in 2019 and commenced training at the prestigious Nigerian Defence Academy as a member of Short Service Combatant Course 46 (Army) on 25 Jan 20. He was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Armoured Corp as a Second Lieutenant on 10 Oct 20 and was the recipient of the Silver Medal for his Cadet Course. He has attended several courses, some of which include Young Officers’ Course Infantry, Mortar Platoon Commanders’ Course and Young Officers’ Course Armour.
Lieutenant Adefemi has served in various units amongst which include 241 Bn, 27TF Bde, and 22 Bde Gar. He is currently serving in Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo as a pioneer Instructor and dedicated to turning out professional and combat ready soldiers for the Nigerian Army. He has participated in various military operations, some of which include, JTF NE-OPHK, Op Desert Sanity II, Op Safe Conduct, and Op Desert Sanity III. Lieutenant Adefemi is happily married and blessed with 2 beautiful kids. His hobbies include reading, football, volleyball, shooting and meeting people.

members.name: Lt Uh Haruna
members.bio:
Lieutenant HARUNA Uzaidu Hamisu was born at Basirka Gwaram, Jigawa state, on 21st Aug 1994, to the family of Alhaji HARUNA Hamisu Ishola and Hajiya Halima Hamisu. He hails from Gwaram Local Government Area of Jigawa State and has a mixed cultural heritage, Mushere from his father’s side and Hausa from his mother’s side.
He began his formal education at Islamiyya Primary School, Barkin Ladi (2001–2006), where he served as Head Boy. He proceeded to Islamic Orientation Secondary school where he obtained secondary school leaving certificate (2006-2012). During his schooling, he actively participated in debate and quiz competitions, and also served as a Head boy which helped develop his communication and analytical skills. In 2013, he gained admission into Federal university Dutse Jigawa State to study Bsc Mathematics where he obtained a First class degree in Bsc Mathematics in 2017.
Upon completion of the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC), he gained admission into the Nigerian Defence Academy as a member of SSC Course 46 and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Armour Corp on 10 October 2020. Since commissioning, he has served in 241 Bn Nguru, 243 Bn Badgary, participating in several operations including Operation HADIN KAI (NE), Operation Mesa and Operation Desert Sanity under Sector 2 OPHK. His appointments include Ag Officer commanding Nguru FOB, Ag Coy 2iC Katarko, 120Bn. He is currently serving as an Instructor at Depot NA Osogbo, and has attended military courses including the Young Officers Course (Armour), Young Officers Course (Infantry),  Regimental and intelligent officer course, which have enhanced his leadership and operational competence.
On a personal note, he enjoys playing football, reading, travelling, and video gaming, which help him maintain physical fitness, mental balance, and personal development.

members.name: Lt Vn Omeje
members.bio:
Lt Vincent Nkemjika Omeje was born on the 2 Feb 1993 into the family of Chief Omeje Vincent Chukwuemeka Emmanuel and Mrs. Omeje Beatrice Mary Nwadimkpa. He hails from Umunocha in Nsukka Local Government Area of Enugu State and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army as Second Lieutenant on 10 October 2020 as a member of Short Service Course 46.
He began his Primary School education at St. Patrick’s Nursery and Primary School, Obollo Afor, Udenu Local Government Area of Enugu State from 1997 - 2003 where he obtained his First School Leaving Certificate and Testimonial. Thereafter, he proceeded to Community Secondary School, Ezimo Uno in Udenu Local Government Area of Enugu State where he obtained his Secondary School Certificate in 2009. He gained admission into the prestigious Federal Polytechnic Kaura Namoda, Zamfara State for Preliminary Studies in 2011 and was later admitted into National Diploma Public Administration and graduated with Upper Credit in 2014. Subsequently, he proceeded for one year industrial training at Kennan Lodge Hotel and Resorts Nsukka. He further gained admission into Akanu Ibiam Federal Polytechnic Unwana Afikpo Ebonyi State in Higher National Diploma Public Administration and graduated with Upper Credit in 2017. He was posted to 3 Bde Kano State for one year National Youth Service Corps in 2018 where he served as the Bde Corper Liaison Officer.
Lt Vincent has attended Leadership and Regimentation Course, Serti Baruwa, Regimental Signal Officers Course, NASI Jaji, Armour Young Officers Course, NAAS Bauchi, Young Officers Course Infantry, NASI Jaji and Tank Weapon Instructor Course, NAAS Bauchi.
Lt Omeje has the following Operational Experience which include; Operation HADIN KAI in Yobe State from 2021 - 2024.
Lt Vincent has served in the following formations and held appointments commiserate to his rank, notably are:
• BN RSO and BN Int Offr 241 Recce Model BN - Nguru, Yobe State.
• BN Adjutant and BN Int Offr 221 Li Tk BN - Kainji, Niger State.
Currently, Lt VN Omeje is a Pioneer Instructor at Depot NA Osogbo and the Ag 2Ic E Trg Coy. He holds a Post Graduate Diploma in Public Policy and Administration from prestigious Bayero University Kano. He is a Member of Rotaract Club D9125.
Lt VN Omeje is married to Mrs. Vincent Patience Nkemjika and the union is blessed with a child. At his leisure he enjoys Reading, Playing Badminton, Squash Football, Meditating and Travelling.

members.name: Lt Col Em Ukande'S
members.bio:
ACTING COMMANDER RECRUITS CENTRE – LIEUTENANT COLONEL EMMANUEL MANASSEH UKANDE
Lieutenant Colonel Emmanuel Manasseh Ukande is a member of Short Service Course 33 of the Nigerian Defence Academy and commissioned in 2003. The senior officer is of the Infantry Corps and has attended military courses commensurate to his rank in the Nigerian Army as well as other specialized warfare courses at Nigerian Army School of Infantry and abroad. Some of these include the Junior and Senior Courses at AFCSC Jaji (January -June 2015 and January 2019 – August 2020 respectively), Commanding Officers Course (January – April 2026), Company Commanders Course (September – December 2015) and Young Officers Course (February – May 2006). Others include Special Operations Command Course in People’s Liberation Army China at the Quanzhou Military Academy (August 2016 – July 2017), Basic and Advanced Airborne Courses at Airborne Wing Jaji (February – March 2005 and April – June 2026), Protection of Civilians Course at Martin Luther Agwai Leadership and Peace Keeping Centre Jaji (July – August 2016). The senior officer also attended United Nations Battalion Course in South Korea (November 2022).
Ukande has served and held several appointments cutting across staff, command and instruction in units and various operations. Some of these include Adjutant and Operations Officer at 192 Battalion (2003 – 2005), Platoon Commander in UNMIL (2006) and UNAMID (2007) respectively. Emmanuel was also an instructor and Administrative Officer at the Tactics Wing of the Nigerian Army School of Infantry (May – August 2016). Other appointments held by the officer include acting Sector Commander in Operations RESTORE ORDER III, BOYONA and ZAMAN LAFIYA (July 2011 – May 2016). He was the Training Officer at the Nigerian Army Special Forces School Buni Yadi (September 2020 – May 2021). He was privileged to command the 2 Special Women Operations Battalion Abakaliki (May 2021 – July 2023) and 196 Amphibious Battalion Baga in Operation HADIN KAI NORTH EAST/MULTINATIONAL JOINT TASK FORCE (July 2023 – May 2025
Ukande is the recipient of awards and decorations which include the Meritorious Service Star, Force Service Star, Passed Staff Course, Nigeria Centenary and Golden Jubilee medals as well as General Operations and Command medals. The officer enjoys working out and observing people. He also delights in mentoring subordinates and impacting knowledge and values. The senior officer is married with children. Lieutenant Colonel Emmanuel Manasseh Ukande was appointed as the pioneer acting Commander Recruits Centre of Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo on 5 June 2025.

members.name: Lt Mb Abdullahi
members.bio:
Lieutenant Muhammad Bello Abdullahi was born at Murtala Mohammed Specialist Hospital, Kano, on 10th May 1996, to the family of Alhaji Abdullahi Abubakar and Hajiya Maimuna Mohammed. He hails from Gwale Local Government Area of Kano State and has a mixed cultural heritage, Hausa from his father’s side and Fulani from his mother’s side. He lost his father at the age of six and was raised by his mother, whose resilience and faith played a significant role in shaping his upbringing. As the eldest of three siblings, he grew up with a strong sense of responsibility, discipline, and respect.
He began his formal education at Badar Nursery and Primary School, Kano (2002–2008), where he served as Head Boy. He proceeded to Badar College for his junior secondary education (2008–2010) and Government Secondary School Gwale for his senior secondary education (2010–2013). During his schooling, he actively participated in debate and quiz competitions, which helped develop his communication and analytical skills. In 2013, he gained admission into Al-Qalam University, Katsina, where he studied Natural and Applied Chemistry and graduated in 2017 with a Bachelor of Science degree.
Upon completion of the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC), he gained admission into the Nigerian Defence Academy as a member of SSC Course 46 and was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Corps of Artillery on 10 October 2020. At his Passing Out Parade, he distinguished himself by winning the Gold Medal, Indian Shield, and Sword of Honour in recognition of outstanding academic and military performance. Since commissioning, he has served in 322 Artillery Regiment, Benin, and 331 Artillery Regiment, Biu, participating in several operations including Operation HADIN KAI (NE), Operation Nut Cracker (NC), and Operation Desert Sanity under Sector 2 OPHK. His appointments include Adjutant 322 Arty Regt, Acting OC Task Team Miringa, and Acting OC Responsive Area Kwaya Kusar. He is currently serving as an Instructor at Depot NA Osogbo, and has attended military courses including the Young Officers Course (Artillery), Young Officers Course (Infantry), Operation and Maintenance officers Course, and Troop Commander Course (Artillery), which have enhanced his leadership and operational competence.
On a personal note, he enjoys playing football, reading, travelling, and video gaming, which help him maintain physical fitness, mental balance, and personal development.

Maj RA Yaornyi is happily married to Mrs Awekine Doris Yaornyi and the union is blessed with 4 children, 3 girls and a boy. He enjoys playing lawn tennis, driving, watching sport and reading.

departments.name: Signals Squadron
departments.brief:
The Signals Wing was established on 6 June 2025 following the commencement of recruit training at Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. Its mandate is to provide effective and efficient communications support to the Depot and to instruct recruits in signals communication, information and communication technology.

The wing is structured into Signals Wing Headquarters, a Communications Training Department and a Communications Support Department. Due to current manpower limitations, these departments are integrated and function as a single entity. The wing's current strength is 1 x 9, against an establishment of 7 x 32.
departments.hod_bio:
Captain Aliyu Muhammed Bello was born on 31 December 1993 into the family of Mr and Mrs Bello Mohammed. He hails from Birnin Kebbi Local Government Area of Kebbi State and is the second of four children.

He attended Turaki International School Kaduna (FSLC 2004) for his primary education and Essence International School Kaduna (WASSCE 2010) for his secondary education. He was admitted to the Nigerian Defence Academy as a member of 64 Regular Course on 22 September 2012 and graduated with a Bachelor of Engineering in Electrical and Electronics Engineering in 2017. He subsequently obtained a Master of Technology in Telecommunication and Electronics Engineering from Obafemi Awolowo University Ile-Ife.

He was commissioned into the Nigerian Army Signals Corps as a Second Lieutenant on 16 September 2017.

He has served in the following appointments: 535 Signals Regiment Jos; Adjutant, HQ Nigerian Army Signals Regiment Lagos; HQ Joint Task Force North East, Op HADIN KAI (December 2022 – March 2025); 562 Brigade Signals Yenagoa; and Acting Senior Instructor, Signals Wing, Depot NA Osogbo (June 2025 – current).

Courses attended include Young Officers Course Signals, Young Officers Course Infantry, Reverse Engineering Malware Analysis Course, Open-Source Intelligence Course and Junior Course, Armed Forces Command and Staff College Jaji.

He is married. His hobbies include reading history and current affairs, playing video games, squash and watching football.

members.name: Capt Am Bello Biography
members.bio:
AMB/DEPOT NA/ OS/G1/25
Capt Aliyu Muhammed Bello was born on 31 Dec 93 into the family of Mr and Mrs Bello Mohammed and hails from Birnin Kebbi Local Government Area of Kebbi State. He is the second of 4 children. Aliyu began his primary school Education
in 1996 at Turaki International School Kaduna where he obtained First School Leaving Certificate in the year 2004. He further proceeded to Essence International School Kaduna where he obtained the West African Senior School Certificate in the year 2010. Capt Bello was subsequently admitted into the prestigious Nigerian Defence Academy Kaduna as a member of 64 Regular Course
on 22 Sep 12. He later graduated with a Bachelor of Engineering in Electrical and Electronics Engineering in 2017.
Capt AM Bello was commissioned into the NA as a Second Lieutenant on 16
Sep 17 and posted to the Nigerian Army Signals Corps. He was subsequently posted to 535 Signals Regiment Jos where he participated in various competitions for the Regiment and won laurels. Bello was later posted to Headquarters Nigerian Army Signals Regiment Lagos where he served as the unit Adjutant for about 2 years. In Dec 22, he was posted to Headquarters Joint Task Force (North East) Operation Hadin Kai where he served successfully until Mar 25 when he proceeded to 562 Brigade Signals Yenagoa. Having served in Yenagoa for about 3 months, Bello was subsequently posted to Depot NA Osogbo as a member of the pioneer team mandated to train competent soldiers for the NA.
During the course of his career, Aliyu attended various military courses commensurate to his rank. They include Young Officers Course Signals, Young Officers Course Infantry, Reverse Engineering Malware Analysis and Open-Source Intelligence Courses amongst others. Similarly, Aliyu attended Obafemi Awolowo University Ile-Ife where he obtained a Master of Technology degree in Telecommunication and Electronics Engineering. Capt Bello has also attended Junior Course at the Armed Forces Command and Staff College Jaji.
Capt AM Bello is happily married. During his leisure, he enjoys reading history,
current affairs, playing video games, squash and watching football.
AM BELLO
Osogbo								Capt
Jun 26								Ag SI Sigs Wg

departments.name: Tactics Wing
departments.hod_bio:
Major A.D. Mohammed was born on 9 June 1985 into the family of the late Alhaji and Hajia Hauwau Mohammed Bida in Kaduna State. He hails from Bida Local Government Area of Niger State.

He attended LEA Katsina Road Primary School, Kaduna (1990 – 1996) for his primary education and Army Secondary School Kaduna (1997 – 2002) for his secondary education. He holds a National Diploma and Higher National Diploma in Mass Communication from Kaduna Polytechnic.

He was enlisted into the Nigerian Army on 10 August 2010 and was commissioned into the Infantry Corps as a member of Short Service Course 39 on 6 April 2011.

He has served in the following units and appointments: Infantry Corps Centre (ICC); Platoon Commander, 103 Battalion; AHQ Special Operations Battalion; 133 Special Forces Battalion; 65 Battalion; 81 Division Task Force Battalion; 5 Battalion; and Acting Senior Instructor, Tactics Wing, Depot NA Osogbo (current).

Courses attended include Special Warfare Course (Tracker); Young Officers Course Infantry; Anti-Tank Platoon Commander Course; Armoury Management Course; Junior Command and Staff Course; and Company Commander Course.

His operational experience includes Operation ZAMAN LAFIYA; Operation LAFIYA DOLE; Operation TURA TAKAI BANGO; Operation RESCUE FINALE; Operation DEEP PUNCH II; Operation HARBIN KUNAMA; Operation MESA; Operation HADIN KAI; and Operation DELTA SAFE. His awards include the Op Lafiya Dole Medal, Op HADIN KAI Medal, General Operations Medal, Force Service Star and Passed Junior Staff Course.

He is married and the union is blessed with three children. His hobbies include basketball, table tennis and badminton.

members.name: Biography Maj Ad Mohammed

Maj AD Mohammed (N/13981) was born on 9 June 1985 to the family of Late Alh and Haj Hauwau Mohammed Bida in Kaduna State. He hails from Bida LGA of Niger State. He obtained his first school living certificate in LEA Katsina road primary school Kaduna between 1990 and 1996. He attended Anny secondary school Kaduna between 1997 and 2002.
He proceeded to Kaduna polytechnic where he obtained a National and Higher National Diploma in Mass communication. He was enlisted into the Nigerian Army on the 10 August 2010 and completed his cadet training on 6 April 2011 as a member of Short Service Course 39.
At the end of his cadet training, Maj AD Mohammed was posted to ICC and subsequently to 103 Bn as a platoon commd. Other units he has served includes, AHQ Special Ops Bn, 133 SF Bn, 65 Bn, 81 Div TF Bn, 5 Bn and now Depot as the Ag SI Tactics. Some of the Ops he participated include, Op Zaman Lafiya, Ops Lafiya Dole, Op Tura Takai Bango, Op Rescue Finale, Op Deep Punch II, Op Harbin Kunama, Op MESA, Op Hadin Kai and Op Delta safe amongst others.
Maj AD Mohammed has attended the following couses. Special Warfare course (Tracker), Young officers course infantry, Anti-Tank platoon comd course, Amoury management course, Junior Comd and Staff Course and Company Comd Course. departments.name: Training Branch
departments.brief: The Training Branch is the central coordinating authority for all recruit training programmes within Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo. It designs, supervises and evaluates the military training curriculum delivered to recruits, ensuring compliance with Nigerian Army training standards and directives. The branch coordinates with all training wings — Drill, Weapons, Tactics, Physical Training and Signals — to produce well-trained soldiers fit for operational duties.

members.name: Capt Fb Goni
members.bio:
BIOGRAPHY ON CAPT FB GONI (N/17344)
Captain Faruq Baba Goni was born on 7 May 1993 to the family of Alhaji Baba Goni at Garki General Hospital, Abuja, FCT. He hail from Potiskum Local Government Area of Yobe State. He is the last in the family of eight children. He started my primary school education in 1998 at Army Children School Asokoro, Abuja where he obtained my First School Leaving School Certificate in 2004. He then proceeded to Army Day Secondary School Asokoro, Abuja where he obtained my Junior Secondary School Certificate in 2007. Thereafter, he attended Command Secondary School, Jos, Plateau State, where he obtained my West African School Certificate in 2011. He was admitted into the Nigerian Defence Academy (NDA) on 21 September 2013 as an officer cadet of 65th Regular Course and graduated in 2017 with a Bachelor of Science in Psychology. He was commissioned Second Lieutenant on 8 October 2018 and posted to the Nigerian Army Armoured Corps (NAAC).
Goni was posted to HQ NAAC where hewas redeployed to 221 Light Tank Battalion, Kainji, Niger State as a Platoon Commander. Thereafter, he was posted to Sector 2 Garrison NE (OPHK) where he was deployed to 25 Brigade, Damboa as a platoon commander, later posted to 211 Demonstration Battalion, Bauchi State as Company Second in Command. Nigerian Army Armour School, Bauchi State as an Instructor. He has  participated in several Operations which are Operation Tura Takai Bango (OPTT) in 2021, Operation Desert Sanity in 2022, Operation Flush in 2022.
Goni have attended courses in order to enhance my professional knowledge. He attended Leadership and Regimentation Course in 2018, Serti-Baruwa, Taraba State, Young Officers’ Course (Infantry) in 2019, Jaji, Kaduna State, Basic French Course in 2020, Sobi, Kwara State, Young Officers’ Course (Armour) in 2021, Bauchi State, Legal Aspect of Internal Security Operations: Nigeria Perspective Course, Basawa, Kaduna State. Armour Platoon Commanders’ Course in 2023, Bauchi State.
He relish a number of activities in the military. Some of these are mess activities like the ‘tankers night’, field training exercises and several operations. I am a Muslim by religion, I’m Married and my hobbies are reading, playing badminton and swimming.

Captain Jeremiah Nanlung Goni, is an officer of the Nigerian Army. He was born on 23rd August 1995 to the family of Dr and Mrs Ishaya Goni at Jos University Teaching Hospital Jos, Plateau State. He hail from Langtang North Local Government Area of Plateau State. I am first in the family of Three children. He started primary school education in 1999 at Hope Christian School Gindiri where he obtained my First School Leaving School Certificate in 2006. He then proceeded to Boys Secondary School Gindiri for my secondary education and got a Senior School Leaving Certificate in 2012. He was admitted into the Nigerian Defence Academy (NDA) on 27 September 2014 as an officer cadet of 66th Regular Course and graduated in 2018 with a Bachelor of Science in Political science and Defence Studies. He was commissioned Second Lieutenant on 5 October 2019 and posted to Infantry Corps Centre (ICC).
Capt Goni was posted to 199 SF Bn from March 2020 to April 2023 during which he served as the Unit’s Operations Officer. Thereafter he was posted to 5 Bn Nigerian Army from May 2023 to June 2025 where he served as the Battalion Adjutant. In March 2026 he was posted to Depot Nigerian Army Osogbo as an instructor where he is serving to date. He has participated in several Operations which include Operation Tura Takai Bango (OPTT) in 2021, Operation Desert Sanity in 2022 and Operation Delta Safe from 2023 to 2025.
Goni have attended courses in order to enhance my professionalism. He has attended Leadership and Regimentation Course in 2019 at 241 Recce Bn Nguru, Young Officers’ Course (Infantry) in 2021at Nigerian Army School of Infantry (NASI), Machine Gun Platoon Commanders’ Course in 2022 at NASI, Special Forces Team Commander’s Course in 2023 At Special Forces Training School, Bakloh India as well as other seminars and lectures.
